title: REST API v2 rootless - PublishAllPorts not working
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Container created with PublishAllPorts=true does not bind ports

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create nginx container and publish all ports
```
podman --log-level=debug system service -t 0 tcp:localhost:8880 2>&1>/tmp/podman-rest.log &

curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/create?name=nginx' --data '{"name":"nginx","Image":"nginx:alpine", "HostConfig":{"PublishAllPorts":true}}'
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/nginx/start'
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Port binding is missing (checked with netstat and by inspecting container)
```
HostConfig": {
            "Binds": [],
            "CgroupMode": "private",
            "ContainerIDFile": "",
            "LogConfig": {
                "Type": "",
                "Config": null
            },
            "NetworkMode": "slirp4netns",
            "PortBindings": {},
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
Port binding is created
```
        "HostConfig": {
            "Binds": [],
            "CgroupMode": "private",
            "ContainerIDFile": "",
            "LogConfig": {
                "Type": "",
                "Config": null
            },
            "NetworkMode": "slirp4netns",
            "PortBindings": {
                "80/tcp": [
                    {
                        "HostIp": "",
                        "HostPort": "39995"
                    }
                ]
            },
```
Please note host port will be most probably different

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.1.0
```

comments.body:
Strongly suspect this is already fixed on master thanks to the work from
@baude

On Wed, Oct 21, 2020 at 7:45 PM psakar <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)*

>
> curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/create?name=nginx' --data '{"name":"nginx","Image":"nginx:alpine", "HostConfig":{"PublishAllPorts":true}}'
> curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" 'http://127.0.0.1:8880/containers/nginx/start'
>
> *Describe the results you received:*
> Port binding is missing (checked with netstat and by inspecting container)

> HostConfig": {

> *Describe the results you expected:*
> Port binding is created

> .

I tried to test it with master, but I'm not able to run my test using testcontainers against podman built from master - attempt to start the container results in 
```
 Request Failed(Internal Server Error): open io.max: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error 
{"cause":"OCI runtime command not found error","message":"open io.max: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error","response":500}
``` 

I was able to reproduce this error using commands mentioned above in the issue description.

I've tested with master running service as root - that is working (tested on FC32 just know updated). Running podman service in rootless mode results in above mentioned error (even when selinux is disabled). Error message is same as descibed in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7938 
@mheon would it be possible to cherry pick the fix you have mentioned in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8105#issuecomment-714005461 (if yes which commits) ?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
